title: Steve Kerr explains how the Celtics went from an ‘afterthought’ to a ‘real favorite’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Steve Kerr praises Celtics' growth, calling them a 'real favorite' after sweeping Warriors in season series.
Jayson Tatum's absence allowed Celtics' younger players to gain experience, contributing to the team's unexpected success.
Celtics excel in offensive efficiency and spacing, key factors in their strong Eastern Conference position.

title: Advocates sue Worcester over response to mental health 911 calls, alleging discrimination
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Advocates filed a federal lawsuit against Worcester, claiming the 911 program discriminates by sending armed police to all mental health calls.
The lawsuit highlights incidents where Worcester police allegedly escalated situations, including restraining children with autism and fracturing one boy's arm.
Less than 25% of Worcester police officers have crisis intervention training; a Mental Health Crisis Response Team was disbanded after 19 months.

title: Hilary Duff will be Northeastern University’s 2026 commencement speaker
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Hilary Duff announced as Northeastern University's 2026 commencement speaker in a Y2K-themed social media post.
Northeastern's commencement ceremony will occur on April 29, 2026, at Fenway Park.
Duff is known for her role in 'Lizzy Maguire' and her 2003 hit song 'What Dreams Are Made Of.'

title: 3 roster needs Patriots must still address after free-agency spending spree
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots seek a proven No. 1 wide receiver, with A.J. Brown as a potential trade target.
New England aims to bolster its pass rush after losing K'Lavon Chaisson to the Commanders.
Concerns over tackle depth prompt Patriots to consider draft options for bolstering their offensive line.

title: Needham 16-year-old charged with murder in Roxbury slaying of woman, 61
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A 16-year-old from Needham is charged with murder in the October shooting of a 61-year-old woman in Roxbury.
The teen is facing charges of murder, aggravated assault, and unlawful possession of a firearm, Boston police reported.
Police continue to investigate and urge anyone with information on Marie Molea-Anthony's death to contact them.

title: Chelsea man released decades after murder conviction won’t be retried, prosecutors say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Thomas Rosa Jr., convicted of 1985 murder, was released in 2020 after 34 years due to new evidence.
Suffolk County DA dropped charges against Rosa citing new forensic evidence, lost evidence, and case law changes.
Rosa, 100th exoneree in Massachusetts, supports others wrongfully convicted through the Exoneree Network.

title: Former Attleboro man gets 35-40 years in prison for raping 5-year-old niece
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: William Machado sentenced to 35-40 years for raping his 5-year-old niece in Attleboro.
Convictions include child rape, indecent assault, and attempted drugging for sexual intercourse.
Machado, located in Chicago, was extradited to Massachusetts for trial after fleeing the state.

title: A month after tragic shooting, Colin Dorgan, Blackstone Valley hockey triumph to win R.I. state title
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Blackstone Valley hockey team won their first Rhode Island Division II title against Lincoln High with a 3-2 victory.
Senior Colin Dorgan tied the game in regulation and contributed a game-winning overtime goal in earlier semifinals.
Tragedy struck when Dorgan's family members were killed in a shooting incident during the team's 'Senior Night' game.

title: Bruins call up former 1st-round pick Lukas Reichel on emergency basis
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins recalled forward Lukas Reichel from Providence Bruins on an emergency basis for their game against the Winnipeg Jets.
Reichel, a former Chicago first-round pick, was acquired from Vancouver Canucks in exchange for a 2026 sixth-round pick.
In three games with Providence, Reichel scored five points, aiming to boost the Bruins' struggling forward lineup.

title: World Cup games at Gillette no longer in jeopardy after Foxborough officially approves license
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Foxborough approved the event license for seven World Cup games at Gillette Stadium after security funding concerns were resolved.
Kraft Sports & Entertainment Group agreed to cover security costs, ensuring the games remain at Gillette as originally scheduled.
A traffic management plan and enhanced MBTA services are included to accommodate 21,000 patrons per game.
